title: campanula cervicaria var. dalmatica
intro: Campanula cervicaria var. dalmatica, also known as Dalmatian bellflower, is a flowering plant species belonging to the campanulaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Dalmatian bellflower is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
